summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Primary Member Validation feature. All 8 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend UI elements verified working correctly.

issues: Minor: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (console warning)

Minor: Nested

inside

causing hydration warning - AlertDialogDescription contains

and

elements

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/companies/{id}/members returns 409 when trying to add second primary member
Backend: POST /api/companies/{id}/members with replace_primary=true replaces existing primary
Backend: PUT /api/companies/{id}/members/{member_id} returns 409 when setting is_primary_contact=true with existing primary
Backend: PUT /api/companies/{id}/members/{member_id} with replace_primary=true replaces existing primary
Backend: 409 response contains correct structure (message, existing_primary with id/name/email, options array)
Backend: Adding member without is_primary_contact succeeds normally
Backend: Updating existing primary member (other fields) keeps primary status
Backend: Adding first primary to company without existing primary succeeds
Frontend: Edit button visible in members table (data-testid='edit-member-{id}')
Frontend: Edit dialog pre-fills member data (name, email, phone, DOB, primary status)
Frontend: Edit dialog title shows 'Edit Member - {Company Name}'
Frontend: 'Update Member' button appears instead of 'Add Member' in edit mode
Frontend: Primary Contact Conflict dialog appears when adding member with is_primary_contact=true
Frontend: Conflict dialog shows existing primary member info (name, email)
Frontend: Conflict dialog has 'Skip (Keep as Regular Member)' and 'Replace Primary Contact' buttons
critical_code_review_comments: Primary member validation correctly implemented with 409 conflict response
Atomic replace of primary contact working - old primary demoted when new primary added with replace_primary=true
Edit member dialog correctly pre-fills all member data including is_primary_contact checkbox
Frontend properly handles 409 response and shows conflict dialog with Skip/Replace options

Minor HTML nesting issue in AlertDialogDescription -

contains

and

elements (cosmetic, doesn't affect functionality)
seed_data_creation: Used existing company 'To Be Honest Circle LLP' (id: 84b0ae6a-d0fb-4139-9cfe-4c5e68f8cabd) with member 'Vikram Kukreja' (id: 1288f840-4816-48bb-a4ae-c4d3aa4d00f8) as primary contact. Test members created with 'TEST_' prefix and cleaned up after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Primary Member Validation feature fully implemented and tested. Key behaviors: 1) POST /api/companies/{id}/members with is_primary_contact=true returns 409 if primary exists (unless replace_primary=true), 2) PUT /api/companies/{id}/members/{member_id} with is_primary_contact=true returns 409 if another primary exists (unless replace_primary=true), 3) 409 response includes existing_primary info and options array ['skip', 'replace'], 4) Frontend shows conflict dialog with Skip/Replace buttons, 5) Edit member dialog pre-fills all data including primary status and shows 'Update Member' button.

backend_api.add_member_conflict: POST /api/companies/{id}/members returns 409 with existing_primary info when is_primary_contact=true and primary exists
backend_api.add_member_replace: POST /api/companies/{id}/members with replace_primary=true demotes existing primary and creates new primary
backend_api.update_member_conflict: PUT /api/companies/{id}/members/{member_id} returns 409 when setting is_primary_contact=true with existing primary
backend_api.update_member_replace: PUT /api/companies/{id}/members/{member_id} with replace_primary=true replaces existing primary

frontend_ui.edit_button: Visible in members table with data-testid='edit-member-{member_id}'
frontend_ui.edit_dialog_prefill: Pre-fills name, email, phone, aadhar, pan, DOB, seat_number, is_primary_contact
frontend_ui.update_button: 'Update Member' button instead of 'Add Member' in edit mode
frontend_ui.conflict_dialog: Shows 'Primary Contact Already Exists' with existing primary info and Skip/Replace buttons
